data.title: $100 Invested In Cal-Maine Foods 20 Years Ago Would Be Worth This Much Today
data.description:
Cal-Maine Foods (NASDAQ:CALM) has outperformed the market over the past 20 years by 8.42% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 16.91%. Currently, Cal-Maine Foods has a market capitalization of $3.69 billion.

Buying $100 In CALM: If an investor had bought $100 of CALM stock 20 years ago, it would be worth $2,284.17 today based on a price of $77.52 for CALM at the time of writing.

Cal-Maine Foods's Performance Over Last 20 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.

This article was generated by Benzinga's automated content engine and reviewed by an editor.

data.title: Why SanDisk Stock Is Rising Monday On AI Memory Buzz
data.description:
That product launch matters for flow-driven traders because the Roundhill Memory ETF (BATS:DRAM) lists Sandisk at a 4.66% weight, alongside Samsung at 25% and Micron at 24%.

Sandisk's move also comes after a volatile memory tape tied to Alphabet's TurboQuant narrative, which initially spooked investors with claims it could cut memory requirements by 6x.

That earlier shakeout set up a rebound bid in names like Sandisk as the market reassessed whether efficiency"actually reduces total memory demand.

  • SanDisk stock is among today’s top performers. Why are SNDK shares rallying?

New DRAM ETF Adds Fresh Attention To AI Memory Names

Roundhill recently launched the Roundhill Memory ETF, a concentrated fund built around the idea that memory chips are becoming a "key constraint" for AI as training and inference workloads scale. The ETF's focus list highlights memory suppliers including Micron, Samsung, and Sandisk, tying the stock to a fresh product-driven attention cycle in semiconductors.

The broader market is trading in a risk-on posture today, with the Russell 2000 up 0.27%, the Nasdaq up 0.32%, and the S&P 500 up 0.12%.

Sandisk Holds Near 52-Week High As Traders Watch Resistance

Technical Analysis At $722.20, Sandisk is holding near the top of its 52-week range, which is consistent with buyers defending a strong uptrend. With moving averages and RSI/MACD readings listed as N/A, the chart read leans more on range positioning and prior turning points than on indicator confirmation.

The stock's 12-month gain of 2179.51% is an extreme run and is backward-looking, but it helps explain why pullbacks can be sharp when momentum cools. The last 52-week high was set on 2026-03-20 at $777.60, so price is still trading close enough that traders often watch for either a retest or a failed rally.

  • Key Resistance: $777.50 — near the prior peak area where rallies have recently stalled.

Sandisk's NAND Footprint Keeps It In The AI Memory Trade

Sandisk is one of the five largest suppliers of NAND flash memory semiconductors globally, and it's vertically integrated through manufacturing sites across Japan via a joint-venture framework with Kioxia. It then repackages much of that flash into SSDs used in consumer devices, external storage, and cloud storage.

That matters for today's ETF-driven attention because "memory" in the AI stack isn't just one product category—investors often basket NAND and DRAM exposure together when they're positioning for AI infrastructure demand. Sandisk's 2025 spin-off from Western Digital also means the stock can trade more "pure-play" on memory-cycle expectations than it did inside a larger HDD/flash mix.

April 30 Earnings Report Becomes Sandisk's Next Big Catalyst

Following last quarter’s results, investors are now tracking the path toward the next reporting date on April 30.

  • EPS Estimate: $13.72 (Up from a loss of 30 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $4.59 Billion (Up from $1.70 Billion YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $574.67. Recent analyst moves include:

  • B of A Securities: Buy (Raises Target to $900.00) (March 23)
  • Citigroup: Buy (Raises Target to $875.00) (March 19)
  • KGI Securities: Initiated with Outperform (Target $992.00) (March 18)

SNDK Shares Edge Higher Monday

SNDK Price Action: SanDisk shares were up 3.84% at $728.51 at the time of publication on Monday. The stock is approaching its 52-week high of $777.60, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Has Bitcoin Reached A Bottom? Watch These Key Accumulation Zones
data.description:
Bitcoin's (CRYPTO: BTC) multi-week consolidation has raised questions about whether the market has already formed a bottom.

One prominent analyst outlines a data-driven framework to identify high-probability accumulation zones ahead of a potential bull cycle.

On-Chain Metrics Signal Strong Support

Crypto chart analyst Ali Martinez pointed to the UTXO Realized Price Distribution (URPD), which shows where Bitcoin last moved on chain.

A large cluster of holders accumulated BTC between $63,111 and $70,685, indicating a strong support zone where investors may defend their positions.

A long-term trendline, in place for nearly a decade, is also being retested in the $56,000 to $60,000 range.

Historically, each touch of this level has preceded major rallies, including gains of 963% in 2017, 261% in 2018, 1,126% in 2020 and 660% in 2022.

Additional indicators reinforce this outlook.

The Cumulative Value Days Destroyed (CVDD), which tracks the transfer of coins from long-term holders to new participants, currently sits near $47,960, a level often seen as a structural base.

The MVRV 0.8 band, around $43,647, represents a zone of extreme market stress where sellers tend to exhaust themselves and long-term investors accumulate. Meanwhile, the long-term holder realized price, near $49,387, is viewed as a key support level.

A break below this could signal a deeper capitulation phase, with downside risk toward the -0.2 standard deviation band near $36,657.

Spreading Capital Is The Strategy

Rather than attempting to time the exact bottom, Martinez recommends spreading capital across multiple high-confluence zones.

These lower ranges are described as "generational buy" areas where long-term investors have historically accumulated heavily.

With sentiment still cautious and many investors on the sidelines, current conditions resemble past phases that preceded major accumulation opportunities.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: This $599 MacBook Could Be Apple's Smartest Move Yet
data.description:
Analysts See New Category

Counterpoint Research said on Monday that it expects Apple to reshape the low-cost laptop market with its MacBook Neo, using its in-house A18 Pro chip to deliver a premium, fanless device at a $599 price point.

The firm believes this move creates a new "entry-premium" category, allowing Apple to compete in a price segment it has historically avoided while driving higher volumes, especially in areas like education, and supporting its broader strategy of ecosystem expansion and services monetization.

Counterpoint expects the Apple MacBook Neo to drive a sevenfold surge in the sub-$700 market.

IDC analyst Nabila Popal recently said Apple is making an aggressive move into the sub-$700 PC market with the MacBook Neo, aiming to capture new users without compromising its premium feel.

The analyst noted the device delivers a strong user experience despite lower pricing, appealing especially to students and first-time buyers, while helping Apple expand its reach, grow market share, and strengthen long-term ecosystem loyalty.

Wall Street Sees Earnings Upside

Bank of America Securities maintained a Buy rating with a $320 price forecast on Apple and sees the MacBook Neo as a key growth driver, marking the company's first major push into the lower-end PC market.

The firm expects the launch to boost Mac revenue and earnings, with most of the contribution incremental, and estimates that even a modest market-share gain in 2026 could add to Apple's earnings per share.

According to calculations by Bank of America, if Apple captures a 10% market share in 2026 while maintaining operating margins of 19%, it could contribute an additional 3 cents to earnings per share.

Technical Analysis

At $256.50, Apple's chart is best read through nearby levels because the 20-day and 100-day simple moving averages (SMAs) —the stock's average price over the last 20 and 100 sessions —are not available in the provided data, so trend confirmation from those benchmarks is currently limited. In that setup, traders tend to lean more on horizontal support and resistance, as well as prior turning points, to gauge whether buyers are defending key areas.

The golden cross in September (September 15, 2025) marked a longer-term trend tailwind at the time, but the stock later broke below support on March 13, 2026, which suggests the uptrend has been challenged and the price is still working through overhead supply. With the most recent swing low on January 20, 2026, and a swing high on February 6, 2026, the current range behavior points to a market still deciding whether that rebound can extend.

Apple is up 41.03% over the last 12 months, showing strong longer-term performance, even though it doesn't predict the next move. Within the 52-week range ($169.21 to $288.62), the stock is below its prior peak, consistent with a consolidation phase rather than a fresh breakout attempt.

  • Key Resistance: $280.50 — a prior ceiling where rallies have tended to stall.
  • Key Support: $255.50 — a nearby floor buyers may try to defend on pullbacks.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the April 30, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: $1.93 (Up from $1.65 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $109.03 Billion (Up from $95.36 Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 32.4x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $306.08. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Wedbush: Outperform (Maintains Target to $350.00) (March 31)
  • Wedbush: Outperform (Maintains Target to $350.00) (March 27)
  • Morgan Stanley: Overweight (Maintains Target to $315.00) (March 23)

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because AAPL carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

Price Action

AAPL Price Action: Apple shares were trading up 0.95% at $258.35 at the time of publication on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: US-Iran War Updates April 6: IRGC Claims Strikes On US, Israeli Ships, Iran Rejects Talks, Israel Kills IRGC Intel Chief (UPDATED)
data.description:
Here are the latest developments in the U.S.–Israel–Iran war on Monday, as the conflict that began on February 28 enters its sixth week.

President Donald Trump, on Sunday, threatened to destroy Iran's energy and civilian infrastructure if it failed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by his deadline of Tuesday, warning of severe consequences in a Truth Social post.

Earlier, he also confirmed the successful rescue of a second crew member after Iran downed a U.S. F-15E fighter jet.

Meanwhile, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy said the Strait of Hormuz will not return to its previous state, especially for the U.S. and Israel, signaling rejection of Trump's call to reopen it. The force added that it is finalizing operational plans for a "new order" in the Persian Gulf.

IDF Says IRGC Oil Chief Killed

The IDF said it killed Mohammad Reza Ashrafi Kahi, head of commerce at the IRGC's Oil Headquarters in Tehran, in an airstrike. He oversaw oil operations generating billions annually, which funded Iran's military buildup and supported proxy groups like the Houthis, Hezbollah, and Hamas.

Regional Mediators Push For 45-Day Truce: Report

Iran-France Call On War Fallout

Iran's Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi spoke with France's Foreign Minister, Jean-Noël Barrot, to discuss escalating regional tensions and the security and economic fallout of U.S. and Israeli military actions against Iran. Both sides emphasized concerns over instability and the need for continued diplomatic engagement.

Iran Drone Hits Fujairah Telecom Site

Authorities in Fujairah are responding after a drone launched from Iran struck a building belonging to telecom company Du. No injuries have been reported, and officials are managing the situation while urging the public to rely on official updates, reported Al Jazeera.

Iraq Rushes Oil Export Plans After Hormuz Exemption

Iraq's state oil marketer SOMO has asked buyers to submit crude oil lifting schedules within 24 hours after Iran reportedly exempted Iraq from transit restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz. The move is aimed at ensuring smooth and stable oil exports, with Iraq confirming all loading terminals are fully operational, though uncertainty remains over tanker movement amid ongoing regional tensions, reported Reuters.

Iran Slams Strike On Water Facility

The Atomic Energy Organization of Iran (AEOI) condemned a joint US-Israeli strike on its Khondab heavy water facility, calling it a "crime against science and human health." Despite the damage, Iran said its scientific progress will continue. The International Atomic Energy Agency confirmed the plant was hit on March 27 and is now non-operational.

Seoul To Send Envoys To Secure Oil Supplies

South Korea plans to send special envoys to Saudi Arabia, Oman, and Algeria to secure crude oil supplies, alongside deploying five Korean-flagged vessels to Saudi Arabia as part of efforts to strengthen energy security, reported Al Jazeera.

Photo courtesy: Shutterstock
